channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Karmine Corp Eliminate GIANTX From LEC Spring Playoffs
item.content:encoded:
Karmine Corp swept GIANTX 3-0 to lock in their place in the LEC Spring Split playoffs 2026 top three.

KCorp will now face their rivals Movistar KOI in an exhilarating match-up with MSI qualification on the line.

And reigning champions G2 Esports will await the eventual winners in the LEC Spring final to defend their title.

This result also means that MKOI has secured a spot for the League of Legends portion of the Esports World Cup.

Meanwhile, GIANTX have been eliminated from the LEC Playoffs before reaching the finals weekend yet again.

GIANTX shocked regular season leaders Team Vitality 3-0 just a day prior, but were then comfortably second best versus KCorp.

GIANTX Crumble Under The Pressure

Coming off the back of a 3-0 victory over the first seeds, all eyes were on GIANTX to see if it was a fluke or not.

The organisation showed a lot of faith in its players by keeping the same five-man roster from last year.

However, in this series the players appeared a little too eager to repay that faith by constantly overreaching.

The series could have been very different had GIANTX held onto their lead in the first game using their unique draft with Mel and Shen bot.

GIANTX were four kills up to nothing and held a 2k gold just eight minutes in, but weren’t patient enough.

They wasted too many resources, including Shen ult, for a failed gank top, which led to Oh ‘Noah’ Hyeon-taek being dove on the other side of the map.

GIANTX then finally killed Kim ‘Canna’ Chang-dong with a dive of their own, but support Yoon ‘Jun’ Se-jun died to turret aggro.

GIANTX were clearly anxious and kept making mistakes. A Baron rush didn’t work, the team unnecessarily committed a double tp for a bot gank that was already a sure kill.

Canna and Yike played teamfights beautifully and punished GIANTX wasting resources to win the first game.

And from there, KCorp were simply a level above the now low-confidence GIANTX and stomped the next two games to end the series.

What are your series score predictions for KCorp vs MKOI? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, the LPL Playoffs have descended into chaos with Team WE recording multiple upsets.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Where Dolls Hang Brings Island Of The Dead Dolls To Gaming
item.content:encoded:
Where Dolls Hang is a psychological survival horror game from developer Steelkrill Studio, inspired by the real-life Island of the Dead Dolls in Mexico.

In Where Dolls Hang, you play as a detective who is looking for details on the hikers who’ve gone missing in the forest. It’s also the location where your daughter went missing, herself.

“What begins as a professional investigation becomes personal,” the game’s description reads. “Somewhere in the forest lies the truth behind the disappearances…and the fate of your daughter that also went missing in the region.”

Players must examine bodies, collect clues, place evidence markers, and document all of their findings using their camera. There are also elements of base building with your safe room, crafting your weapons and tools, and even boat customization.

Steelkrill Studio calls the area you’re investigating a “vibrant yet disturbing forest ecosystem”. A dynamic day/night cycle can change gameplay, making it easier or more difficult to hide and avoid creatures. Speaking of creatures, they hunt during the day. However, once nightfall happens, they become even more dangerous.

Check out the reveal trailer for Where Dolls Hang below. The game is scheduled to launch sometime in 2026 on PC via Steam.

What do you think of Where Dolls Hang? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out the Insider Access first reveal of co-op survival horror game CODE EXIT. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Tarkov’s Icebreaker Raid Timer Increased Following Community Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has increased the duration of the raid timer on Icebreaker, the all-new map, to 50 minutes, following feedback from the community. The folks enjoying the extra-hard extraction challenge cried out for a longer raid timer that allows for more complete looting, a higher chance of solving the map’s mysteries, and a longer window to take down the map’s boss, Wedge.

BSG has just confirmed that the raid timer increase has been put in place due to popular demand.

Icebreaker Just Got Longer

Icebreaker has proven to be relatively divisive in Escape from Tarkov. It’s a phenomenal map that offers a unique way to fight, as well as a new boss, a story chapter, and some fresh loot items, but it’s painfully challenging and almost a no-go for casual gamers.

In recent days, Battlestate Games has tweaked the difficulty of the game’s boss and Black Division enemies to make things a little more forgiving, but defeating Wedge still requires a bit of cheese.

Now, in a post on social media, BSG’s Design Lead, Sergei Domontovich, confirmed the latest change to hit Icebreaker:

This is a marked improvement over the previous timer, which sat at a meager 30 minutes. By the time players had breached the Icebreaker ship, worked their way through the various levels, completed missions, engaged in tough fights, and dug up loot, they barely had enough time to summon the chopper to get out of the map again.

That is, of course, assuming they can get into the map in the first place. The prerequisites and method for reaching Icebreaker are pretty tough, and again, a bit of a blocker for casual fans.

Do you think this change is worthwhile? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Delta Force is preparing a UGC drive

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Streets Of Rage Movie Has Found Its Writers & Director
item.content:encoded:
The Streets of Rage movie is officially moving forward, as the long-awaited adaptation of SEGA’s iconic beat ’em up franchise has locked in both its writers and director.

Taking on screenplay duties are Pat Casey and Josh Miller, the writing duo behind the successful Sonic the Hedgehog films. They replace Derek Kolstad, best known for the John Wick franchise, who penned an earlier version of the script after Lionsgate first acquired the rights to Streets of Rage in 2022.

Directing the project will be Jeymes Samuel, whose previous credits include The Harder They Fall. Samuel will join a production team of Dmitri Johnson, Toru Nakahara, Michael Lawrence Goldberg, and Timothy I. Stevenson. Notably, Johnson also worked on the Sonic movies, though the Streets of Rage film was always his goal.

More On The Streets Of Rage Film

The production update comes via Variety, though details about the movie itself remain limited. Plot information has yet to be revealed, but the film is reportedly set to lean heavily into the cult-classic series’ signature elements, including its memorable characters, acclaimed electronic soundtrack, and fast-paced beat ’em up action.

Speaking about the creative team behind the adaptation, Lionsgate Motion Picture Group chair Erin Westerman expressed confidence that Samuel is the perfect fit for the project:

We and our partners at Sega knew that ‘Streets of Rage’ demanded a bold filmmaker. Jeymes Samuel’s visceral and explosive style—not to mention his signature use of unforgettable music—is perfect for audiences both familiar with and new to the series. We are so excited for how he will bring his own memorable take to this beloved video game IP.

With a director, writers, and producers now firmly in place, the Streets of Rage movie is beginning to take shape, although fans will likely have to wait a little longer before learning more about its story, cast, or release window.

Will you be keeping the Streets of Rage movie on your radar? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about how an ex-Bioware dev thinks product placement could fund games, as well as the rumors that a Baldar’s Gate 2 Remake is in development.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Ex-BioWare Dev Suggests Product Placement to Fund Games
item.content:encoded:
In a recent video, a former BioWare dev and Dragon Age boss discussed ‘product placement’ as a way for video games to raise/recover development funds.

The new video posted by former BioWare veteran Mark Darrah comes with a boatload of information and insights. Most recently, we covered Darrah’s point that AAA gaming has become too dependent on live-service titles. Now, he has suggested alternative ways game devs can recover costs.

Product Placement Could Be a Way for Games to Make Money, Says Dragon Age Boss

During a recent video posted on the ‘Mark Darrah on Games’ YouTube channel, spotted by IGN, Darrah discussed how video games should steal the business models of movies. Darrah began by explaining how the live-action Smurfs movie was able to fund itself through product placement. According to the former BioWare veteran, this allowed the movie to be made at no cost, as revenue from product placements covered all expenses.

He then discusses the issue of excessive reliance on microtransactions, suggesting that games should explore new opportunities and alternative revenue streams. Darrah mentioned, “Product placement is a very small part of video games right now compared to movies and television. Maybe it could be a larger part of development. Maybe there are relationships there to be formed.”

Product placement is present in video games; however, Darrah recommends a deeper integration of these elements into game development. This approach would enhance their relevance within the property and likely make advertisers more willing to invest money. However, not all games might agree with this approach.

In other news, PlayStation has caught the neck of a major shovelware publisher, leading to the delisting of over 1,000 games. Additionally, the California Assembly has passed the Protect Our Games Act by a vote of 43-16, meaning that the bill will move to the California State Senate. What are your thoughts on increasing product placement in video games to bring more money? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3’s gameplay trailer. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
